content:
# 🚨 MANDATORY: Team Memory System 🚨

A shared team memory stores critical gotchas, conventions, and debugging insights across sessions.

## REQUIRED: /recall BEFORE Starting Any Task

You MUST run /recall at the start of every task to check for relevant prior knowledge. Skipping this risks repeating known mistakes and wasting time on already-solved problems.

```
/recall "<topic or keyword relevant to your task>"
```

Examples:
- Working on Stripe integration? → /recall "Stripe"
- Fixing a database query? → /recall "database" or /recall "Drizzle"
- Debugging a test? → /recall "testing patterns"
- Working on team creation? → /recall "team" or /recall "createId"
- Touching waivers? → /recall "waiver"

**Do NOT skip this step.** Past sessions have stored critical gotchas that will save significant debugging time.

## REQUIRED: /remember After Discoveries

When you discover something useful — a gotcha, root cause, convention, or debugging insight — you MUST save it immediately with /remember. Do not wait until the end of the session.

```
/remember "<what you learned>" --category=gotcha|convention|debugging|architecture|workflow --priority=critical|moderate|ephemeral
```

## Summary

- **BEFORE work**: /recall to load relevant memories (MANDATORY)
- **DURING work**: /remember as soon as you discover something worth saving (MANDATORY)
- Failure to use team memory means the team loses knowledge and repeats mistakes.

## deepwiki
DeepWiki MCP provides AI-powered documentation for GitHub repositories.

Available tools:
- read_wiki_structure: Get a list of documentation topics for a repository
- read_wiki_contents: View full documentation about a repository
- ask_question: Ask any question about a repository and get an AI-powered answer
- list_available_repos: List your available repositories (private mode only)
- generate_wiki: Generate a codebase wiki for a repository — only use when explicitly requested by the user (private mode only)
- devin_knowledge_manage: Manage Devin knowledge notes and suggestions — list, search, get, create, update, delete notes, view folder structure, list/view/dismiss knowledge suggestions (private mode only)
- devin_playbook_manage: Manage Devin playbooks — list, get, create, update, delete (private mode only)
- devin_schedule_manage: Manage scheduled Devin sessions — list, get, create, update, delete (private mode only)
- devin_session_create: Create one or more child Devin sessions (private mode only)
- devin_session_interact: Manage a Devin session — get status, send messages, sleep/terminate/archive, read messages & attachments, manage tags (private mode only)
- devin_session_events: Inspect session events — list summaries, fetch full details, or search event contents (private mode only)
- devin_session_search: Search and filter Devin sessions (private mode only)
- list_integrations: List all native integrations and MCP servers with their status and settings URLs (private mode only)

## plugin:linear:linear
When passing string values to tools, send the content directly without escape sequences. For example, use real newlines in markdown content rather than literal backslash-n (\n) characters.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- agent-browser: Browser automation CLI for AI agents. Use when the user needs to interact with websites, including navigating pages, filling forms, clicking buttons, taking screenshots, extracting data, testing web apps, or automating any browser task. Triggers include requests to "open a website", "fill out a form", "click a button", "take a screenshot", "scrape data from a page", "test this web app", "login to a site", "automate browser actions", or any task requiring programmatic web interaction.
- seo-aeo-best-practices: SEO and AEO best practices for metadata, Open Graph, sitemaps, robots.txt, hreflang, JSON-LD structured data, EEAT, and content optimized for search engines and AI answer surfaces. Use this skill when implementing page SEO, technical SEO, schema markup, international SEO, AI-overview readiness, or improving content for Google, ChatGPT, Perplexity, and similar assistants.
- self-improving-agent: A universal self-improving agent that learns from ALL skill experiences. Uses multi-memory architecture (semantic + episodic + working) to continuously evolve the codebase. Auto-triggers on skill completion/error with hooks-based self-correction.
- web-design-guidelines: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to "review my UI", "check accessibility", "audit design", "review UX", or "check my site against best practices".
- linear: Manage issues, projects & team workflows in Linear. Use when the user wants to read, create or updates tickets in Linear.
- breadboarding: Transform a workflow description into affordance tables showing UI and Code affordances with their wiring. Use to map existing systems or design new ones from shaped parts.
- repo-autopsy: Clone a GitHub repo into a local cache and perform deep analysis using full local toolchain (rg, ast-grep, git, fd, tokei, gitleaks). Use when the user wants to inspect, explore, audit, or understand an external GitHub repository — structure, dependencies, hotspots, exports, secrets, code stats, git blame, or targeted file reads. Triggers on phrases like "autopsy", "analyze this repo", "clone and explore", "what's inside owner/repo", or any request to investigate a third-party GitHub project without adding it to the current working tree.
- adr-skill: Create and maintain Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) optimized for agentic coding workflows. Use when you need to propose, write, update, accept/reject, deprecate, or supersede an ADR; bootstrap an adr folder and index; consult existing ADRs before implementing changes; or enforce ADR conventions. This skill uses Socratic questioning to capture intent before drafting, and validates output against an agent-readiness checklist.
- breadboard-reflection: Breadboard Analysis
- stripe-best-practices: Best practices for building Stripe integrations
- frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- vercel-react-best-practices: React and Next.js performance optimization guidelines from Vercel Engineering. This skill should be used when writing, reviewing, or refactoring React/Next.js code to ensure optimal performance patterns. Triggers on tasks involving React components, Next.js pages, data fetching, bundle optimization, or performance improvements.
- vercel-composition-patterns: React composition patterns that scale. Use when refactoring components with boolean prop proliferation, building flexible component libraries, or designing reusable APIs. Triggers on tasks involving compound components, render props, context providers, or component architecture. Includes React 19 API changes.
- shaping: Use this methodology when collaboratively shaping a solution with the user - iterating on problem definition (requirements) and solution options (shapes).
- tanstack-start-server-only: TanStack Start server-only patterns using createServerOnlyFn and cloudflare:workers env access.
Use when: accessing Cloudflare environment variables (APP_URL, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, etc.),
creating server-only utility functions, working with Stripe or other server-only APIs,
or when you need to ensure code only runs on the server and throws if called from client.
Covers createServerOnlyFn vs createServerFn differences, env access patterns, and centralized
server-only utilities.
- documentation: Centralizes Diataxis documentation framework guidance so Claude can write, review, or improve documentation following the four distinct types (tutorials, how-to guides, reference, explanation).
- athlete-docs: Documentation guidance for competition athletes and volunteers in WODsmith. Use when writing, reviewing, or improving athlete-facing documentation including registration, scheduling, workout viewing, leaderboards, check-in, and volunteer coordination.
- ralph-mode: Autonomous AI coding loop that ships features while you sleep. Use when the user wants to run Ralph, start an autonomous coding session, work overnight, or let Claude implement multiple user stories autonomously. Triggers on "ralph", "autonomous mode", "overnight coding", "autonomous loop", or requests to implement multiple stories without supervision.
- server-fn-auth: Authentication and authorization patterns for TanStack Start server functions.
Use when: adding auth to a new server function, fixing missing authorization,
checking user permissions, protecting endpoints, or implementing organizer/volunteer
access control for competitions.
- test: Primary entry point for testing guidance. Routes to appropriate test type (unit, integration, E2E) based on what you're testing. Use when asked about "test", "testing", "write tests", "TDD", or test strategy. Embeds Testing Trophy philosophy and TDD Red-Green-Refactor as core discipline.
- claim-url-lookup: Look up claim URLs for placeholder users created via manual registration. Use when needing to find the sign-up claim link for a specific email address, debug claim token issues, or verify claim tokens exist in local KV. Triggers on "claim URL", "claim token", "claim link", "placeholder user sign-up link", or requests to find the registration invite link for an email.
- competition-organizer-docs: Generate and review documentation for competition organizers using Diataxis framework. Use when writing docs for organizer workflows like creating competitions, managing divisions, scheduling heats, handling registrations, entering results, or managing volunteers/judges.
- type-safety: Fix type assertions and improve TypeScript type safety. Use when encountering 'as unknown as' casts, manual type definitions that duplicate schema types, or unclear type errors in database queries, especially with Drizzle ORM relations. Also use when verifying types
- date-timezone: Handle date and timezone conversions correctly in forms and displays. Use when working with: Date inputs, new Date(), formatDate, toLocaleDateString, getDate(), getMonth(), getFullYear(), date parsing, UTC conversion, timezone issues, dates displaying wrong day, date-only fields in forms, YYYY-MM-DD parsing. Triggers: dates off by one day, timezone bugs, form date handling, competition dates, event dates, calendar dates.
- integration-test: Guide for writing integration tests with Vitest and Testing Library. Use when testing multi-component workflows, database interactions, React components with context providers, or full user flows. Covers the Testing Trophy philosophy (integration > unit), factory patterns for test data, MSW for network mocking, async testing patterns (waitFor, findBy), and custom render with providers. Use this for tests that cross multiple modules or layers of the application.
- permissions: Guide for working with team-based permissions and authorization in the WODsmith codebase. Use when touching TEAM_PERMISSIONS constants, hasTeamPermission/requireTeamPermission functions, adding permission checks to actions or server functions, creating features requiring authorization, or ensuring client-server permission consistency.
- unit-test: Guide for writing unit tests with Vitest. Use when writing tests for service functions, pure logic, or webhook handlers. Covers TDD Red-Green-Refactor cycle, Arrange-Act-Assert pattern, anti-patterns, pure function testing, and mocking at boundaries.
- tanstack-start-boundaries: TanStack Start server/client code boundary patterns. Use when: debugging virtual module resolution errors (tanstack-start-injected-head-scripts, tanstack-start-manifest, #tanstack-router-entry), importing from @tanstack/react-start/server, using getCookie/getRequestHeaders/getRequestUrl, adding providers to router.tsx, or when Vite fails to resolve TanStack Start imports. Covers createServerFn patterns, dynamic imports, and proper placement of server-only code.
- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills using TypeScript/Bun scripts (Windows-compatible). Use when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Claude's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations.
- drag-and-drop: Implement drag and drop using @atlaskit/pragmatic-drag-and-drop. Use when implementing sortable lists, reorderable items, kanban boards, or any drag-drop interactions. Covers draggable setup, drop targets, edge detection, drag previews, and critical state management patterns to avoid performance issues.
- my-pr-comments: Loads the pr review comments for the current branch.
- review: Centralizes persona-driven code reviews (Carmack, React core, Anders, React Router, Next.js, etc.) so Claude can pick or combine expert viewpoints when the user asks for a code review or perspective-specific critique. Use when user asks for code review, architecture feedback, or expert perspective on implementation.
- local-db: Handle local database schema changes with Drizzle and PlanetScale. Use when making schema changes to src/db/schema.ts, adding/modifying database tables or columns, or when asked about database migrations. Covers the push-based local development workflow and when to generate migrations.
- e2e-test: End-to-end testing with Playwright. Use when writing E2E tests, implementing page objects, setting up test fixtures, handling authentication in tests, adding visual regression testing, or configuring E2E tests for CI. Covers when to use E2E vs integration tests, critical path identification, page object pattern, test isolation, and CI considerations.
- team-memory: Store, search, and manage team memory from AI coding sessions. Use /remember to save observations (conventions, gotchas, debugging tips, architecture decisions, workflows). Use /recall to search memories by semantic query. Use /feedback to rate memory quality (helpful/harmful/irrelevant). Triggers on any request to save, recall, search, or rate team knowledge.
- alchemy-cloudflare: Alchemy IaC patterns for deploying TanStack Start apps to Cloudflare Workers with PlanetScale databases via Hyperdrive. Use when setting up new TanStack Start projects, configuring Alchemy deployments, working with PlanetScale/Drizzle migrations, local development with Cloudflare bindings, or deploying to custom domains.
- logging: Observability logging patterns for server functions in TanStack Start on Cloudflare Workers. Use when: adding logging to server functions, debugging request flows, tracking entity CRUD operations, correlating logs across requests, adding observability to new features, troubleshooting request tracing, working with PostHog/OpenTelemetry logs. Triggers: logInfo, logError, logWarning, withRequestContext, addRequestContextAttribute, logEntityCreated, request tracing, observability, correlation IDs.
- create-pr: Create a PR from current changes with PRD gif
- sync-diagrams: Store and synchronize digrams with codebase in ai/diagrams directory
- linear-project: Work on a Linear project with structured workflow
- test-pr: Review PR changes and provide comprehensive test coverage recommendations using unit, integration, and e2e testing skills
- fix-lint: /fix-lint
- swarm:inbox: Check swarm mail inbox for messages from other agents
- swarm:hive: Query and manage swarm tasks (cells)
- swarm:status: Check swarm coordination status - workers, messages, reservations
- swarm:swarm: Decompose a task into parallel subtasks and coordinate execution
- swarm:handoff: Properly end a swarm session - release reservations, sync state, generate continuation prompt
- swarm:swarm-coordination: Multi-agent coordination patterns for OpenCode swarm workflows. Use when work benefits from parallelization or coordination.
- swarm:always-on-guidance: Always-on rule-oriented guidance for claude-plugin agents. Use to align behavior, tool usage, and model-specific defaults (GPT-5.2-code vs Opus 4.5) while avoiding deprecated bd/cass references.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Before starting work, run `lat search` with one or more queries describing the user's intent.
ALWAYS do this, even when the task seems straightforward — search results may reveal critical design details, protocols, or constraints.
Use `lat section` to read the full content of relevant matches.
Do not read files, write code, or run commands until you have searched.

Remember: `lat.md/` must stay in sync with the codebase. If you change code, update the relevant sections in `lat.md/` and run `lat check` before finishing.

"scored", 90 ) 91 const [tiebreak, setTiebreak] = useState( 92 safeDecode(existing?.tieBreakScore, event.tiebreakScheme ?? event.scheme), 93 ) 94 const [isSaving, setIsSaving] = useState(false) 95 96 const handleSave = async () => { 97 setIsSaving(true) 98 try { 99 await saveScore({ 100 data: { 101 competitionId, 102 organizingTeamId, 103 trackWorkoutId: event.trackWorkoutId, 104 workoutId: event.workoutId, 105 registrationId, 106 userId, 107 divisionId, 108 score: scoreInput.trim(), 109 scoreStatus, 110 tieBreakScore: tiebreak.trim() || null, 111 workout: { 112 scheme: event.scheme, 113 scoreType: event.scoreType, 114 timeCap: event.timeCap, 115 tiebreakScheme: event.tiebreakScheme, 116 repsPerRound: event.repsPerRound, 117 roundsToScore: event.roundsToScore, 118 }, 119 }, 120 }) 121 toast.success("Score saved") 122 onSaved?.() 123 router.invalidate() 124 } catch (error) { 125 toast.error( 126 error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Failed to save score", 127 ) 128 } finally { 129 setIsSaving(false) 130 } 131 } 132 133 const isTimeScheme = 134 event.scheme === "time" || event.scheme === "time-with-cap" 135 136 return ( 137

/**
 * Shared types for the athlete detail page co-located components.
 *
 * These mirror the server function return shape for
 * `getOrganizerAthleteDetailFn` — keeping them here avoids circular imports
 * and lets each co-located component stay under its line budget.
 */

export interface AthleteDetailMember {
  userId: string
  firstName: string | null
  lastName: string | null
  email: string
  avatar: string | null
  isCaptain: boolean
  isActive: boolean
  joinedAt: Date | null
}

export interface AthleteDetailRegistration {
  id: string
  status: string
  teamName: string | null
  divisionId: string | null
  paymentStatus: string | null
  paidAt: Date | null
  registeredAt: Date
  metadata: unknown
  commercePurchaseId: string | null
  userId: string
  athleteTeamId: string | null
}

export interface AthleteDetailDivision {
  id: string
  label: string
  teamSize: number
}

export interface AthleteDetailCommercePurchase {
  id: string
  totalCents: number
  status: string
  completedAt: Date | null
  stripePaymentIntentId: string | null
}

export interface AthleteDetailPendingInvite {
  id: string
  email: string
  status: string
  guestName?: string
  createdAt: Date
  expiresAt: Date | null
  pendingAnswers?: Array<{ questionId: string; answer: string }>
  pendingSignatures?: Array<{
    waiverId: string
    signedAt: string
    signatureName: string
  }>
  submittedAt?: string
  affiliateName?: string
}

export interface AthleteDetailQuestion {
  id: string
  label: string
  type: string
  helpText: string | null
  // Stored as a JSON string in DB (or null); parse when rendering selects.
  options: string | null
  required: boolean
  forTeammates: boolean
  sortOrder: number
}

export interface AthleteDetailWaiver {
  id: string
  title: string
  required: boolean
}

export interface AthleteDetailWaiverSignature {
  userId: string
  waiverId: string
  signedAt: Date
}

export interface AthleteDetailEvent {
  id: string
  trackWorkoutId: string
  workoutId: string
  workoutName: string
  scheme: string
  scoreType: string | null
  timeCap: number | null
  tiebreakScheme: string | null
  repsPerRound: number | null
  roundsToScore: number | null
  submissionWindowStartsAt: Date | string | null
  submissionWindowEndsAt: Date | string | null
  ordinal: number
}

export interface AthleteDetailVideoSubmission {
  id: string
  eventId: string
  trackWorkoutId: string
  videoIndex: number
  userId: string
  videoUrl: string
  notes: string | null
  reviewedAt: Date | null
  status: string
  scoreId: string | null
}

export interface AthleteDetailScoreRound {
  roundIndex: number
  scoreValue: number
}

export interface AthleteDetailScore {
  id: string
  trackWorkoutId: string | null
  userId: string
  divisionId: string | null
  scoreStatus: string
  scoreValue: number | null
  secondaryScore: number | null
  tieBreakScore: number | null
  scoreRounds: AthleteDetailScoreRound[]
  updatedAt: Date
}

export function memberDisplayName(member: AthleteDetailMember): string {
  const name = [member.firstName, member.lastName]
    .filter(Boolean)
    .join(" ")
    .trim()
  return name || member.email
}

export function memberInitials(member: AthleteDetailMember): string {
  const first = member.firstName?.[0] ?? ""
  const last = member.lastName?.[0] ?? ""
  return (first + last).toUpperCase() || member.email[0]?.toUpperCase() || "?"
}

1	/**
2	 * Competition Score Server Functions for TanStack Start
3	 * Port from apps/wodsmith/src/server/competition-scores.ts and
4	 * apps/wodsmith/src/actions/competition-score-actions.ts
5	 *
6	 * OBSERVABILITY:
7	 * - All score operations are logged with entity IDs for tracing
8	 * - Score saves/updates track competitionId, userId, scoreId
9	 * - Permission checks are logged for audit trails
10	 * - Batch operations include counts and error summaries
11	 */
12	
13	import { createServerFn } from "@tanstack/react-start"
14	import { and, eq, gt, inArray, isNull, ne, or } from "drizzle-orm"
15	import { z } from "zod"
16	import { getDb } from "@/db"
17	import {
18	  addRequestContextAttribute,
19	  logEntityDeleted,
20	  logEntityUpdated,
21	  logError,
22	  logInfo,
23	  logWarning,
24	  updateRequestContext,
25	} from "@/lib/logging"
26	import { getEvlog } from "@/lib/evlog"
27	import {
28	  type CompetitionHeat,
29	  competitionEventsTable,
30	  competitionHeatAssignmentsTable,
31	  competitionHeatsTable,
32	  competitionRegistrationsTable,
33	  competitionsTable,
34	  competitionVenuesTable,

36	} from "@/db/schemas/competitions"
37	import { entitlementTable } from "@/db/schemas/entitlements"
38	import {
39	  programmingTracksTable,
40	  trackWorkoutsTable,
41	} from "@/db/schemas/programming"
42	import { scalingLevelsTable } from "@/db/schemas/scaling"
43	import { scoreRoundsTable, scoresTable } from "@/db/schemas/scores"
44	import { teamMembershipTable } from "@/db/schemas/teams"
45	import { userTable } from "@/db/schemas/users"
46	import {
47	  SCORE_STATUS_VALUES,
48	  type ScoreStatus,
49	  type ScoreType,
50	  type TiebreakScheme,
51	  type WorkoutScheme,
52	  workouts,
53	} from "@/db/schemas/workouts"
54	import {
55	  computeSortKey,
56	  decodeScore,
57	  encodeRounds,
58	  encodeScore,
59	  getDefaultScoreType,
60	  type WorkoutScheme as ScoringWorkoutScheme,
61	  STATUS_ORDER,
62	  sortKeyToString,
63	} from "@/lib/scoring"
64	import { getSessionFromCookie } from "@/utils/auth"
65	import { requireTeamPermission } from "@/utils/team-auth"
66	import { TEAM_PERMISSIONS } from "@/db/schemas/teams"
67	import { aggregateValues } from "@/lib/scoring"
68	
69	// ============================================================================
70	// Types
71	// ============================================================================
72	
73	/** Round score data for multi-round workouts */

164	/**
165	 * Check if current time is within the event's submission window.
166	 * Only applies to online competitions.
167	 */
168	async function isWithinSubmissionWindow(
169	  competitionId: string,
170	  trackWorkoutId: string,
171	): Promise<{ allowed: boolean; reason?: string }> {
172	  const db = getDb()
173	
174	  // Get competition type
175	  const [competition] = await db
176	    .select({
177	      competitionType: competitionsTable.competitionType,
178	    })
179	    .from(competitionsTable)
180	    .where(eq(competitionsTable.id, competitionId))
181	    .limit(1)
182	
183	  if (!competition) {
184	    return { allowed: false, reason: "Competition not found" }
185	  }
186	
187	  // Only check submission windows for online competitions
188	  if (competition.competitionType !== "online") {
189	    return { allowed: true }
190	  }
191	
192	  // Get competition event with submission window
193	  const [event] = await db
194	    .select({
195	      submissionOpensAt: competitionEventsTable.submissionOpensAt,
196	      submissionClosesAt: competitionEventsTable.submissionClosesAt,
197	    })
198	    .from(competitionEventsTable)
199	    .where(
200	      and(
201	        eq(competitionEventsTable.competitionId, competitionId),
202	        eq(competitionEventsTable.trackWorkoutId, trackWorkoutId),
203	      ),
204	    )
205	    .limit(1)
206	
207	  // If no event record exists, allow submission (backward compatibility)
208	  if (!event) {
209	    return { allowed: true }
210	  }
211	
212	  // If no submission window is configured, allow submission
213	  if (!event.submissionOpensAt || !event.submissionClosesAt) {
214	    return { allowed: true }
215	  }
216	
217	  // Check if current time is within the window
218	  const now = new Date()
219	  const opensAt = new Date(event.submissionOpensAt)
220	  const closesAt = new Date(event.submissionClosesAt)
221	
222	  if (now < opensAt) {
223	    return {
224	      allowed: false,
225	      reason: `Submission window opens at ${opensAt.toISOString()}`,
226	    }
227	  }
228	
229	  if (now > closesAt) {
230	    return {
231	      allowed: false,
232	      reason: `Submission window closed at ${closesAt.toISOString()}`,
233	    }
234	  }
235	
236	  return { allowed: true }
237	}

239	/**
240	 * Map ScoreStatus to the simplified status type for scores table.
241	 */
242	function mapToNewStatus(
243	  status: ScoreStatus,
244	): "scored" | "cap" | "dq" | "withdrawn" {
245	  switch (status) {
246	    case "scored":
247	      return "scored"
248	    case "cap":
249	      return "cap"
250	    case "dq":
251	      return "dq"
252	    case "withdrawn":
253	    case "dns":
254	    case "dnf":
255	      return "withdrawn"
256	    default:
257	      return "scored"
258	  }
259	}

332	// Internal Helper: Get heats for a workout
333	// ============================================================================
334	
335	interface HeatWithAssignmentsInternal extends CompetitionHeat {
336	  venue: { id: string; name: string } | null
337	  division: { id: string; label: string } | null
338	  assignments: Array<{
339	    laneNumber: number
340	    registrationId: string
341	  }>
342	}
343	
344	async function getHeatsForWorkoutInternal(
345	  trackWorkoutId: string,
346	): Promise<HeatWithAssignmentsInternal[]> {
347	  const db = getDb()
348	
349	  // If this is a child event, resolve to parent for heat lookup
350	  // (heats are scheduled on parent events, not individual sub-events)
351	  let heatWorkoutId = trackWorkoutId
352	  const trackWorkout = await db
353	    .select({ parentEventId: trackWorkoutsTable.parentEventId })
354	    .from(trackWorkoutsTable)
355	    .where(eq(trackWorkoutsTable.id, trackWorkoutId))
356	    .limit(1)
357	  if (trackWorkout[0]?.parentEventId) {
358	    heatWorkoutId = trackWorkout[0].parentEventId
359	  }
360	
361	  // Get heats
362	  const heats = await db
363	    .select()
364	    .from(competitionHeatsTable)
365	    .where(eq(competitionHeatsTable.trackWorkoutId, heatWorkoutId))
366	
367	  if (heats.length === 0) {
368	    return []
369	  }
370	
371	  // Get venue IDs and division IDs
372	  const venueIds = heats
373	    .map((h) => h.venueId)
374	    .filter((id): id is string => id !== null)
375	  const divisionIds = heats
376	    .map((h) => h.divisionId)
377	    .filter((id): id is string => id !== null)
378	
379	  // Fetch venues
380	  const venues =
381	    venueIds.length > 0
382	      ? await db
383	          .select({
384	            id: competitionVenuesTable.id,
385	            name: competitionVenuesTable.name,
386	          })
387	          .from(competitionVenuesTable)
388	          .where(inArray(competitionVenuesTable.id, venueIds))
389	      : []
390	  const venueMap = new Map(venues.map((v) => [v.id, v]))
391	
392	  // Fetch divisions
393	  const divisions =
394	    divisionIds.length > 0
395	      ? await db
396	          .select({
397	            id: scalingLevelsTable.id,
398	            label: scalingLevelsTable.label,
399	          })
400	          .from(scalingLevelsTable)
401	          .where(inArray(scalingLevelsTable.id, divisionIds))
402	      : []
403	  const divisionMap = new Map(divisions.map((d) => [d.id, d]))
404	
405	  // Fetch assignments for all heats
406	  const heatIds = heats.map((h) => h.id)
407	  const assignments =
408	    heatIds.length > 0
409	      ? await db
410	          .select({
411	            heatId: competitionHeatAssignmentsTable.heatId,
412	            laneNumber: competitionHeatAssignmentsTable.laneNumber,
413	            registrationId: competitionHeatAssignmentsTable.registrationId,
414	          })
415	          .from(competitionHeatAssignmentsTable)
416	          .where(inArray(competitionHeatAssignmentsTable.heatId, heatIds))
417	      : []
418	
419	  // Group assignments by heat
420	  const assignmentsByHeat = new Map<string, typeof assignments>()
421	  for (const assignment of assignments) {
422	    const existing = assignmentsByHeat.get(assignment.heatId) ?? []
423	    existing.push(assignment)
424	    assignmentsByHeat.set(assignment.heatId, existing)
425	  }
426	
427	  // Build result
428	  return heats.map((heat) => ({
429	    ...heat,
430	    venue: heat.venueId ? (venueMap.get(heat.venueId) ?? null) : null,
431	    division: heat.divisionId
432	      ? (divisionMap.get(heat.divisionId) ?? null)
433	      : null,
434	    assignments: (assignmentsByHeat.get(heat.id) ?? []).map((a) => ({
435	      laneNumber: a.laneNumber,
436	      registrationId: a.registrationId,
437	    })),
438	  }))
439	}

445	/**
446	 * Get athletes and existing scores for a competition event
447	 */
448	export const getEventScoreEntryDataFn = createServerFn({ method: "GET" })
449	  .inputValidator((data: unknown) =>
450	    getEventScoreEntryDataInputSchema.parse(data),
451	  )
452	  .handler(async ({ data }): Promise<EventScoreEntryData> => {
453	    const db = getDb()
454	
455	    // Get the track workout (event) with workout details
456	    const [result] = await db
457	      .select({
458	        trackWorkoutId: trackWorkoutsTable.id,
459	        trackOrder: trackWorkoutsTable.trackOrder,
460	        pointsMultiplier: trackWorkoutsTable.pointsMultiplier,
461	        workoutId: workouts.id,
462	        workoutName: workouts.name,
463	        workoutDescription: workouts.description,
464	        workoutScheme: workouts.scheme,
465	        workoutScoreType: workouts.scoreType,
466	        workoutTiebreakScheme: workouts.tiebreakScheme,
467	        workoutTimeCap: workouts.timeCap,
468	        workoutRepsPerRound: workouts.repsPerRound,
469	        workoutRoundsToScore: workouts.roundsToScore,
470	      })
471	      .from(trackWorkoutsTable)
472	      .innerJoin(workouts, eq(trackWorkoutsTable.workoutId, workouts.id))
473	      .where(eq(trackWorkoutsTable.id, data.trackWorkoutId))
474	      .limit(1)
475	
476	    if (!result) {
477	      throw new Error("Event not found")
478	    }
479	
480	    // Restructure to match expected format
481	    const trackWorkout = {
482	      id: result.trackWorkoutId,
483	      trackOrder: result.trackOrder,
484	      pointsMultiplier: result.pointsMultiplier,
485	      workout: {
486	        id: result.workoutId,
487	        name: result.workoutName,
488	        description: result.workoutDescription,
489	        scheme: result.workoutScheme as WorkoutScheme,
490	        scoreType: result.workoutScoreType as ScoreType | null,
491	        tiebreakScheme: result.workoutTiebreakScheme as TiebreakScheme | null,
492	        timeCap: result.workoutTimeCap,
493	        repsPerRound: result.workoutRepsPerRound,
494	        roundsToScore: result.workoutRoundsToScore,
495	      },
496	    }
497	
498	    // Get all registrations for this competition (with optional division filter)
499	    const registrations = await db
500	      .select({
501	        registration: competitionRegistrationsTable,
502	        user: userTable,
503	        division: scalingLevelsTable,
504	      })
505	      .from(competitionRegistrationsTable)
506	      .innerJoin(
507	        userTable,
508	        eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.userId, userTable.id),
509	      )
510	      .leftJoin(
511	        scalingLevelsTable,
512	        eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.divisionId, scalingLevelsTable.id),
513	      )
514	      .where(
515	        and(
516	          eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.eventId, data.competitionId),
517	          ne(competitionRegistrationsTable.status, REGISTRATION_STATUS.REMOVED),
518	          ...(data.divisionId
519	            ? [eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.divisionId, data.divisionId)]
520	            : []),
521	        ),
522	      )
523	
524	    const filteredRegistrations = registrations
525	
526	    // Get existing scores for this event
527	    const userIds = filteredRegistrations.map((r) => r.user.id)
528	    const existingScores =
529	      userIds.length > 0
530	        ? await db
531	            .select()
532	            .from(scoresTable)
533	            .where(
534	              and(
535	                eq(scoresTable.competitionEventId, data.trackWorkoutId),
536	                inArray(scoresTable.userId, userIds),
537	              ),
538	            )
539	        : []
540	
541	    // Get score_rounds for all existing scores
542	    const scoreIds = existingScores.map((s) => s.id)
543	    const existingRounds =
544	      scoreIds.length > 0
545	        ? await db
546	            .select({
547	              scoreId: scoreRoundsTable.scoreId,
548	              roundNumber: scoreRoundsTable.roundNumber,
549	              value: scoreRoundsTable.value,
550	            })
551	            .from(scoreRoundsTable)
552	            .where(inArray(scoreRoundsTable.scoreId, scoreIds))
553	        : []
554	
555	    // Group rounds by scoreId and convert to legacy format
556	    const setsByScoreId = new Map<string, ExistingSetData[]>()
557	    for (const round of existingRounds) {
558	      const existing = setsByScoreId.get(round.scoreId) || []
559	
560	      const scheme = existingScores.find((s) => s.id === round.scoreId)?.scheme
561	      let score: number | null = null
562	      let reps: number | null = null
563	
564	      if (scheme === "rounds-reps") {
565	        const rounds = Math.floor(round.value / 100000)
566	        reps = round.value % 100000
567	        score = rounds
568	      } else if (
569	        scheme === "time" ||
570	        scheme === "time-with-cap" ||
571	        scheme === "emom"
572	      ) {
573	        score = Math.round(round.value / 1000)
574	      } else if (scheme === "load") {
575	        score = Math.round(round.value / 453.592)
576	      } else if (scheme === "meters") {
577	        score = Math.round(round.value / 1000)
578	      } else if (scheme === "feet") {
579	        score = Math.round(round.value / 304.8)
580	      } else {
581	        score = round.value
582	      }
583	
584	      existing.push({
585	        setNumber: round.roundNumber,
586	        score,
587	        reps,
588	      })
589	      setsByScoreId.set(round.scoreId, existing)
590	    }
591	
592	    // Create a map of userId to score
593	    const scoresByUserId = new Map(existingScores.map((s) => [s.userId, s]))
594	
595	    // Get unique divisions for the filter dropdown
596	    const divisionIds = [
597	      ...new Set(
598	        registrations
599	          .map((r) => r.registration.divisionId)
600	          .filter((id): id is string => id !== null),
601	      ),
602	    ]
603	
604	    const divisions =
605	      divisionIds.length > 0
606	        ? await db
607	            .select({
608	              id: scalingLevelsTable.id,
609	              label: scalingLevelsTable.label,
610	              position: scalingLevelsTable.position,
611	            })
612	            .from(scalingLevelsTable)
613	            .where(inArray(scalingLevelsTable.id, divisionIds))
614	        : []
615	
616	    // Get team members for team registrations
617	    const athleteTeamIds = [
618	      ...new Set(
619	        filteredRegistrations
620	          .map((r) => r.registration.athleteTeamId)
621	          .filter((id): id is string => id !== null),
622	      ),
623	    ]
624	
625	    // Fetch team memberships with user info for all athlete teams
626	    const teamMemberships =
627	      athleteTeamIds.length > 0
628	        ? await db
629	            .select({
630	              teamId: teamMembershipTable.teamId,
631	              userId: teamMembershipTable.userId,
632	              firstName: userTable.firstName,
633	              lastName: userTable.lastName,
634	            })
635	            .from(teamMembershipTable)
636	            .innerJoin(userTable, eq(teamMembershipTable.userId, userTable.id))
637	            .where(inArray(teamMembershipTable.teamId, athleteTeamIds))
638	        : []
639	
640	    // Group team members by teamId
641	    const membersByTeamId = new Map<
642	      string,
643	      Array<{
644	        userId: string
645	        firstName: string | null
646	        lastName: string | null
647	      }>
648	    >()
649	    for (const membership of teamMemberships) {
650	      const existing = membersByTeamId.get(membership.teamId) || []
651	      existing.push({
652	        userId: membership.userId,
653	        firstName: membership.firstName,
654	        lastName: membership.lastName,
655	      })
656	      membersByTeamId.set(membership.teamId, existing)
657	    }
658	
659	    // Build athletes array
660	    const athletes: EventScoreEntryAthlete[] = filteredRegistrations.map(
661	      (reg) => {
662	        const existingScore = scoresByUserId.get(reg.user.id)
663	        const scoreSets = existingScore
664	          ? (setsByScoreId.get(existingScore.id) || []).sort(
665	              (a, b) => a.setNumber - b.setNumber,
666	            )
667	          : []
668	
669	        // Get team members if this is a team registration
670	        const athleteTeamId = reg.registration.athleteTeamId
671	        const captainUserId = reg.registration.captainUserId || reg.user.id
672	        const teamMembers: TeamMemberInfo[] = athleteTeamId
673	          ? (membersByTeamId.get(athleteTeamId) || []).map((member) => ({
674	              userId: member.userId,
675	              firstName: member.firstName || "",
676	              lastName: member.lastName || "",
677	              isCaptain: member.userId === captainUserId,
678	            }))
679	          : []
680	
681	        // Sort team members: captain first, then alphabetically by last name
682	        teamMembers.sort((a, b) => {
683	          if (a.isCaptain && !b.isCaptain) return -1
684	          if (!a.isCaptain && b.isCaptain) return 1
685	          return a.lastName.localeCompare(b.lastName)
686	        })
687	
688	        // Decode scores from encoding to display format
689	        let wodScore = ""
690	        let tieBreakScore: string | null = null
691	        let secondaryScore: string | null = null
692	
693	        if (existingScore) {
694	          // Decode primary score
695	          if (existingScore.scoreValue !== null) {
696	            wodScore = decodeScore(
697	              existingScore.scoreValue,
698	              existingScore.scheme as ScoringWorkoutScheme,
699	              { compact: false },
700	            )
701	          }
702	
703	          // Decode tiebreak score if present
704	          if (
705	            existingScore.tiebreakValue !== null &&
706	            existingScore.tiebreakScheme
707	          ) {
708	            tieBreakScore = decodeScore(
709	              existingScore.tiebreakValue,
710	              existingScore.tiebreakScheme as ScoringWorkoutScheme,
711	              { compact: false },
712	            )
713	          }
714	
715	          // Decode secondary score if present
716	          if (existingScore.secondaryValue !== null) {
717	            secondaryScore = String(existingScore.secondaryValue)
718	          }
719	        }
720	
721	        return {
722	          registrationId: reg.registration.id,
723	          userId: reg.user.id,
724	          firstName: reg.user.firstName || "",
725	          lastName: reg.user.lastName || "",
726	          email: reg.user.email || "",
727	          divisionId: reg.registration.divisionId,
728	          divisionLabel: reg.division?.label || "Open",
729	          teamName: reg.registration.teamName || null,
730	          teamMembers,
731	          existingResult: existingScore
732	            ? {
733	                resultId: existingScore.id,
734	                wodScore,
735	                scoreStatus: existingScore.status as ScoreStatus | null,
736	                tieBreakScore,
737	                secondaryScore,
738	                sets: scoreSets,
739	              }
740	            : null,
741	        }
742	      },
743	    )
744	
745	    // Sort by division label, then by team name (or last name for individuals)
746	    athletes.sort((a, b) => {
747	      if (a.divisionLabel !== b.divisionLabel) {
748	        return a.divisionLabel.localeCompare(b.divisionLabel)
749	      }
750	      const aName = a.teamName || a.lastName
751	      const bName = b.teamName || b.lastName
752	      return aName.localeCompare(bName)
753	    })
754	
755	    return {
756	      event: {
757	        id: trackWorkout.id,
758	        trackOrder: trackWorkout.trackOrder,
759	        pointsMultiplier: trackWorkout.pointsMultiplier,
760	        workout: trackWorkout.workout,
761	      },
762	      athletes,
763	      divisions: divisions.sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position),
764	    }
765	  })

767	/**
768	 * Get score entry data with heat groupings for a competition event.
769	 * This extends getEventScoreEntryData with heat assignment information.
770	 */
771	export const getEventScoreEntryDataWithHeatsFn = createServerFn({
772	  method: "GET",
773	})
774	  .inputValidator((data: unknown) =>
775	    getEventScoreEntryDataInputSchema.parse(data),
776	  )
777	  .handler(async ({ data }): Promise<EventScoreEntryDataWithHeats> => {
778	    // Get the base score entry data
779	    const baseData = await getEventScoreEntryDataFn({ data })
780	
781	    // Get heats for this workout
782	    const heatsWithAssignments = await getHeatsForWorkoutInternal(
783	      data.trackWorkoutId,
784	    )
785	
786	    // Build a set of all assigned registration IDs
787	    const assignedRegistrationIds = new Set<string>()
788	    for (const heat of heatsWithAssignments) {
789	      for (const assignment of heat.assignments) {
790	        assignedRegistrationIds.add(assignment.registrationId)
791	      }
792	    }
793	
794	    // Find unassigned registration IDs (athletes in baseData but not in any heat)
795	    const allRegistrationIds = new Set(
796	      baseData.athletes.map((a) => a.registrationId),
797	    )
798	    const unassignedRegistrationIds = [...allRegistrationIds].filter(
799	      (id) => !assignedRegistrationIds.has(id),
800	    )
801	
802	    // Transform heats to simplified format for UI
803	    const heats: HeatScoreGroup[] = heatsWithAssignments.map((heat) => ({
804	      heatId: heat.id,
805	      heatNumber: heat.heatNumber,
806	      scheduledTime: heat.scheduledTime,
807	      venue: heat.venue,
808	      division: heat.division,
809	      assignments: heat.assignments.map((a) => ({
810	        laneNumber: a.laneNumber,
811	        registrationId: a.registrationId,
812	      })),
813	    }))
814	
815	    // Sort heats by number
816	    heats.sort((a, b) => a.heatNumber - b.heatNumber)
817	
818	    return {
819	      ...baseData,
820	      heats,
821	      unassignedRegistrationIds,
822	    }
823	  })

825	/**
826	 * Save a single athlete's competition score
827	 */
828	export const saveCompetitionScoreFn = createServerFn({ method: "POST" })
829	  .inputValidator((data: unknown) =>
830	    saveCompetitionScoreInputSchema.parse(data),
831	  )
832	  .handler(
833	    async ({
834	      data,
835	    }): Promise<{
836	      success: boolean
837	      data: { resultId: string; isNew: boolean }
838	    }> => {
839	      const db = getDb()
840	
841	      getEvlog()?.set({
842	        action: "save_score",
843	        score: {
844	          competitionId: data.competitionId,
845	          registrationId: data.registrationId,
846	          workoutId: data.workoutId,
847	        },
848	      })
849	
850	      // Update request context for tracing
851	      addRequestContextAttribute("competitionId", data.competitionId)
852	      addRequestContextAttribute("trackWorkoutId", data.trackWorkoutId)
853	      addRequestContextAttribute("athleteUserId", data.userId)
854	
855	      logInfo({
856	        message: "[Score] Save competition score started",
857	        attributes: {
858	          competitionId: data.competitionId,
859	          trackWorkoutId: data.trackWorkoutId,
860	          userId: data.userId,
861	          registrationId: data.registrationId,
862	          divisionId: data.divisionId,
863	          scoreStatus: data.scoreStatus,
864	        },
865	      })
866	
867	      // Check submission window for online competitions
868	      const submissionCheck = await isWithinSubmissionWindow(
869	        data.competitionId,
870	        data.trackWorkoutId,
871	      )
872	
873	      if (!submissionCheck.allowed) {
874	        logWarning({
875	          message: "[Score] Submission window blocked",
876	          attributes: {
877	            competitionId: data.competitionId,
878	            trackWorkoutId: data.trackWorkoutId,
879	            userId: data.userId,
880	            reason: submissionCheck.reason,
881	          },
882	        })
883	        throw new Error(
884	          submissionCheck.reason || "Score submission not allowed at this time",
885	        )
886	      }
887	
888	      // Validate workout info is provided
889	      if (!data.workout) {
890	        throw new Error("Workout info is required to save competition score")
891	      }
892	
893	      const scheme = data.workout.scheme as ScoringWorkoutScheme
894	      const scoreType =
895	        (data.workout.scoreType as ScoreType) || getDefaultScoreType(scheme)
896	      const workoutTiebreakScheme =
897	        (data.workout.tiebreakScheme as TiebreakScheme) ?? null
898	
899	      // Encode score using encoding
900	      let encodedValue: number | null = null
901	      let encodedRounds: number[] = []
902	      const hasRoundScores = !!(data.roundScores && data.roundScores.length > 0)
903	
904	      if (data.roundScores && data.roundScores.length > 0) {
905	        // Multi-round: encode each round and aggregate
906	        const roundInputs = data.roundScores.map((rs) => ({ raw: rs.score }))
907	        const result = encodeRounds(roundInputs, scheme, scoreType)
908	        // `encodeRounds` drops rounds that fail to encode — that would
909	        // misalign roundStatuses with the per-round rows we insert below.
910	        if (result.rounds.length !== data.roundScores.length) {
911	          throw new Error("Every round in roundScores must be a valid score")
912	        }
913	        encodedValue = result.aggregated
914	        encodedRounds = result.rounds
915	      } else if (data.score?.trim()) {
916	        // Single score: encode directly
917	        encodedValue = encodeScore(data.score, scheme)
918	      }
919	
920	      // Map client-declared status to simplified type. For multi-round
921	      // `time-with-cap` we derive status server-side from the rounds
922	      // themselves (mirroring `submitVideoFn`), ignoring the client value.
923	      let newStatus = mapToNewStatus(data.scoreStatus)
924	      const roundStatuses: Array<"scored" | "cap"> = []
925	      let cappedRoundCount = 0
926	
927	      if (
928	        scheme === "time-with-cap" &&
929	        data.workout.timeCap &&
930	        hasRoundScores &&
931	        encodedValue !== null
932	      ) {
933	        // Multi-round time cap: per-round inference. Preserve the summed
934	        // total — do NOT clamp to cap. Parent status becomes "cap" if any
935	        // round is capped.
936	        const capMs = data.workout.timeCap * 1000
937	        for (const roundValue of encodedRounds) {
938	          const isRoundCapped = roundValue >= capMs
939	          roundStatuses.push(isRoundCapped ? "cap" : "scored")
940	          if (isRoundCapped) cappedRoundCount++
941	        }
942	        // Preserve terminal statuses (dq/withdrawn). Only flip between
943	        // scored/cap when the caller declared a non-terminal status.
944	        if (newStatus !== "dq" && newStatus !== "withdrawn") {
945	          newStatus = cappedRoundCount > 0 ? "cap" : "scored"
946	        }
947	      } else if (
948	        newStatus === "cap" &&
949	        scheme === "time-with-cap" &&
950	        data.workout.timeCap
951	      ) {
952	        // Single-round legacy clamp + reps-at-cap behavior.
953	        encodedValue = data.workout.timeCap * 1000
954	      }
955	
956	      // Parse secondary score (reps completed at cap) if provided
957	      let secondaryValue: number | null = null
958	      if (data.secondaryScore && newStatus === "cap") {
959	        const parsed = Number.parseInt(data.secondaryScore.trim(), 10)
960	        if (!Number.isNaN(parsed) && parsed >= 0) {
961	          secondaryValue = parsed
962	        }
963	      }
964	
965	      // Store time cap in milliseconds for reference
966	      const timeCapMs = data.workout.timeCap
967	        ? data.workout.timeCap * 1000
968	        : null
969	
970	      // Encode tiebreak if provided (needed for sortKey computation)
971	      let tiebreakValue: number | null = null
972	      if (data.tieBreakScore && data.workout.tiebreakScheme) {
973	        try {
974	          tiebreakValue = encodeScore(
975	            data.tieBreakScore,
976	            data.workout.tiebreakScheme as ScoringWorkoutScheme,
977	          )
978	        } catch (_error) {
979	          // Silently ignore tiebreak encoding errors
980	        }
981	      }
982	
983	      // Compute sort key for efficient leaderboard queries.
984	      // Includes the multi-round `cappedRoundCount` tiebreaker so scores
985	      // with fewer capped rounds sort ahead of scores with more, even
986	      // when the summed total is slower.
987	      const sortKey =
988	        encodedValue !== null
989	          ? computeSortKey({
990	              value: encodedValue,
991	              status: newStatus,
992	              scheme,
993	              scoreType,
994	              cappedRoundCount,
995	              // Include time cap info for capped scores
996	              timeCap:
997	                newStatus === "cap" && timeCapMs && secondaryValue !== null
998	                  ? { ms: timeCapMs, secondaryValue }
999	                  : undefined,
1000	              // Include tiebreak for proper tie-breaking in rankings
1001	              tiebreak:
1002	                tiebreakValue !== null && data.workout.tiebreakScheme
1003	                  ? {
1004	                      scheme: data.workout.tiebreakScheme as "time" | "reps",
1005	                      value: tiebreakValue,
1006	                    }
1007	                  : undefined,
1008	            })
1009	          : null
1010	
1011	      // Get teamId from competition context
1012	      const [teamResult] = await db
1013	        .select({
1014	          ownerTeamId: programmingTracksTable.ownerTeamId,
1015	        })
1016	        .from(trackWorkoutsTable)
1017	        .innerJoin(
1018	          programmingTracksTable,
1019	          eq(trackWorkoutsTable.trackId, programmingTracksTable.id),
1020	        )
1021	        .where(eq(trackWorkoutsTable.id, data.trackWorkoutId))
1022	        .limit(1)
1023	
1024	      if (!teamResult?.ownerTeamId) {
1025	        throw new Error("Could not determine team ownership for competition")
1026	      }
1027	
1028	      const teamId = teamResult.ownerTeamId
1029	
1030	      // Use a transaction for atomicity: upsert score → retrieve ID → manage rounds
1031	      const scoreId = await db.transaction(async (tx) => {
1032	        // Insert/update scores table
1033	        await tx
1034	          .insert(scoresTable)
1035	          .values({
1036	            userId: data.userId,
1037	            teamId,
1038	            workoutId: data.workoutId,
1039	            competitionEventId: data.trackWorkoutId,
1040	            scheme,
1041	            scoreType,
1042	            scoreValue: encodedValue,
1043	            status: newStatus,
1044	            statusOrder: STATUS_ORDER[newStatus],
1045	            sortKey: sortKey ? sortKeyToString(sortKey) : null,
1046	            tiebreakScheme: workoutTiebreakScheme,
1047	            tiebreakValue,
1048	            timeCapMs,
1049	            secondaryValue,
1050	            scalingLevelId: data.divisionId,
1051	            asRx: true,
1052	            recordedAt: new Date(),
1053	          })
1054	          .onDuplicateKeyUpdate({
1055	            set: {
1056	              scoreValue: encodedValue,
1057	              status: newStatus,
1058	              statusOrder: STATUS_ORDER[newStatus],
1059	              sortKey: sortKey ? sortKeyToString(sortKey) : null,
1060	              tiebreakScheme: workoutTiebreakScheme,
1061	              tiebreakValue,
1062	              timeCapMs,
1063	              secondaryValue,
1064	              scalingLevelId: data.divisionId,
1065	              updatedAt: new Date(),
1066	            },
1067	          })
1068	
1069	        // Get the final score ID (either new or existing)
1070	        const [finalScore] = await tx
1071	          .select({ id: scoresTable.id })
1072	          .from(scoresTable)
1073	          .where(
1074	            and(
1075	              eq(scoresTable.competitionEventId, data.trackWorkoutId),
1076	              eq(scoresTable.userId, data.userId),
1077	            ),
1078	          )
1079	          .limit(1)
1080	
1081	        if (!finalScore) {
1082	          throw new Error("Failed to retrieve score after upsert")
1083	        }
1084	
1085	        const id = finalScore.id
1086	
1087	        // Handle score_rounds - delete existing and insert new
1088	        if (data.roundScores && data.roundScores.length > 0) {
1089	          // Delete existing rounds
1090	          await tx
1091	            .delete(scoreRoundsTable)
1092	            .where(eq(scoreRoundsTable.scoreId, id))
1093	
1094	          // Convert and insert new rounds. For multi-round time caps we
1095	          // persist per-round `status` ("cap" / "scored") derived above
1096	          // so the leaderboard can count capped rounds without replaying
1097	          // the cap check, and so the round breakdown UI tags each round.
1098	          const roundsToInsert = data.roundScores.map((round, index) => {
1099	            let roundValue: number
1100	
1101	            if (scheme === "rounds-reps") {
1102	              const roundsNum =
1103	                Number.parseInt(round.parts?.[0] ?? round.score, 10) || 0
1104	              const reps = Number.parseInt(round.parts?.[1] ?? "0", 10) || 0
1105	              roundValue = roundsNum * 100000 + reps
1106	            } else if (
1107	              scheme === "time" ||
1108	              scheme === "time-with-cap" ||
1109	              scheme === "emom"
1110	            ) {
1111	              roundValue = encodeScore(round.score, scheme) ?? 0
1112	            } else {
1113	              roundValue = encodeScore(round.score, scheme) ?? 0
1114	            }
1115	
1116	            return {
1117	              scoreId: id,
1118	              roundNumber: index + 1,
1119	              value: roundValue,
1120	              status: roundStatuses[index] ?? null,
1121	            }
1122	          })
1123	
1124	          await tx.insert(scoreRoundsTable).values(roundsToInsert)
1125	        }
1126	
1127	        return id
1128	      })
1129	
1130	      // Log score entity creation/update
1131	      addRequestContextAttribute("scoreId", scoreId)
1132	      logEntityUpdated({
1133	        entity: "score",
1134	        id: scoreId,
1135	        attributes: {
1136	          competitionId: data.competitionId,
1137	          trackWorkoutId: data.trackWorkoutId,
1138	          userId: data.userId,
1139	          registrationId: data.registrationId,
1140	          divisionId: data.divisionId,
1141	          scoreStatus: data.scoreStatus,
1142	          hasRoundScores: !!(data.roundScores && data.roundScores.length > 0),
1143	        },
1144	      })
1145	
1146	      if (data.roundScores && data.roundScores.length > 0) {
1147	        logInfo({
1148	          message: "[Score] Round scores saved",
1149	          attributes: {
1150	            scoreId,
1151	            roundCount: data.roundScores.length,
1152	          },
1153	        })
1154	      }
1155	
1156	      logInfo({
1157	        message: "[Score] Competition score saved successfully",
1158	        attributes: {
1159	          scoreId,
1160	          competitionId: data.competitionId,
1161	          trackWorkoutId: data.trackWorkoutId,
1162	          userId: data.userId,
1163	          scoreStatus: data.scoreStatus,
1164	        },
1165	      })
1166	
1167	      return { success: true, data: { resultId: scoreId, isNew: true } }
1168	    },
1169	  )

1171	/**
1172	 * Batch save multiple competition scores

1174	export const saveCompetitionScoresFn = createServerFn({ method: "POST" })
1175	  .inputValidator((data: unknown) =>
1176	    saveCompetitionScoresInputSchema.parse(data),
1177	  )
1178	  .handler(
1179	    async ({
1180	      data,
1181	    }): Promise<{
1182	      success: boolean
1183	      data: {
1184	        savedCount: number
1185	        errors: Array<{ userId: string; error: string }>
1186	      }
1187	    }> => {
1188	      const errors: Array<{ userId: string; error: string }> = []
1189	      let savedCount = 0
1190	
1191	      getEvlog()?.set({
1192	        action: "save_scores_batch",
1193	        batch: { competitionId: data.competitionId, count: data.scores.length },
1194	      })
1195	
1196	      // Update request context
1197	      addRequestContextAttribute("competitionId", data.competitionId)
1198	      addRequestContextAttribute("trackWorkoutId", data.trackWorkoutId)
1199	
1200	      logInfo({
1201	        message: "[Score] Batch save competition scores started",
1202	        attributes: {
1203	          competitionId: data.competitionId,
1204	          trackWorkoutId: data.trackWorkoutId,
1205	          scoreCount: data.scores.length,
1206	        },
1207	      })
1208	
1209	      // Check submission window for online competitions
1210	      const submissionCheck = await isWithinSubmissionWindow(
1211	        data.competitionId,
1212	        data.trackWorkoutId,
1213	      )
1214	
1215	      if (!submissionCheck.allowed) {
1216	        logWarning({
1217	          message: "[Score] Batch submission window blocked",
1218	          attributes: {
1219	            competitionId: data.competitionId,
1220	            trackWorkoutId: data.trackWorkoutId,
1221	            reason: submissionCheck.reason,
1222	          },
1223	        })
1224	        throw new Error(
1225	          submissionCheck.reason || "Score submission not allowed at this time",
1226	        )
1227	      }
1228	
1229	      // Get workout info for all scores
1230	      const db = getDb()
1231	      const [workoutResult] = await db
1232	        .select({
1233	          scheme: workouts.scheme,
1234	          scoreType: workouts.scoreType,
1235	          repsPerRound: workouts.repsPerRound,
1236	          roundsToScore: workouts.roundsToScore,
1237	          timeCap: workouts.timeCap,
1238	          tiebreakScheme: workouts.tiebreakScheme,
1239	        })
1240	        .from(workouts)
1241	        .where(eq(workouts.id, data.workoutId))
1242	        .limit(1)
1243	
1244	      if (!workoutResult) {
1245	        throw new Error("Workout not found")
1246	      }
1247	
1248	      for (const scoreData of data.scores) {
1249	        try {
1250	          await saveCompetitionScoreFn({
1251	            data: {
1252	              competitionId: data.competitionId,
1253	              organizingTeamId: data.organizingTeamId,
1254	              trackWorkoutId: data.trackWorkoutId,
1255	              workoutId: data.workoutId,
1256	              registrationId: scoreData.registrationId,
1257	              userId: scoreData.userId,
1258	              divisionId: scoreData.divisionId,
1259	              score: scoreData.score,
1260	              scoreStatus: scoreData.scoreStatus,
1261	              tieBreakScore: scoreData.tieBreakScore,
1262	              secondaryScore: scoreData.secondaryScore,
1263	              workout: workoutResult,
1264	            },
1265	          })
1266	          savedCount++
1267	        } catch (error) {
1268	          const errorMessage =
1269	            error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Unknown error"
1270	          errors.push({
1271	            userId: scoreData.userId,
1272	            error: errorMessage,
1273	          })
1274	          logError({
1275	            message: "[Score] Failed to save individual score in batch",
1276	            error,
1277	            attributes: {
1278	              userId: scoreData.userId,
1279	              registrationId: scoreData.registrationId,
1280	            },
1281	          })
1282	        }
1283	      }
1284	
1285	      // Restore batch-level action after per-item saves overwrote it
1286	      getEvlog()?.set({
1287	        action: "save_scores_batch",
1288	        batch: {
1289	          competitionId: data.competitionId,
1290	          count: data.scores.length,
1291	          saved: savedCount,
1292	          errors: errors.length,
1293	        },
1294	      })
1295	
1296	      logInfo({
1297	        message: "[Score] Batch save competition scores completed",
1298	        attributes: {
1299	          competitionId: data.competitionId,
1300	          trackWorkoutId: data.trackWorkoutId,
1301	          savedCount,
1302	          errorCount: errors.length,
1303	        },
1304	      })
1305	
1306	      return { success: true, data: { savedCount, errors } }
1307	    },
1308	  )
